title: Iran fires largest missile barrage in weeks as Israelis set to sit down for Passover Seder

title: American journalist kidnapped in Baghdad by Iranian proxy
description:
The Iran-backed terror group Kataib Hezbollah, an Iraqi Shiite militia, kidnapped American journalist Shelly Kittleson in downtown Baghdad on March 31. The US government had previously warned Kittleson of a threat against her and the group’s desire to kill or abduct journalists.

title: China and Pakistan present new Iran deal: Ceasefire for opening Hormuz
description: China and Pakistan presented on Tuesday a new initiative for ending the war in Iran that includes an immediate ceasefire and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. The intrigue: Pakistan's foreign minister told Axios the plan came together during his visit to Beijing on Tuesday. Asked whether he supports the new initiative, President Trump declined to comment on the specifics but told Axios the diplomacy with Iran was going well. It's unclear if

title: Interim Venezuelan president ousts defense chief as Washington makes limited progress in Caracas
description: On March 18, Venezuelan acting President Delcy Rodríguez removed longtime Defense Minister Vladimir Padrino López and installed Gustavo González López, followed by initiating a broad restructuring of the military’s top leadership. These moves unfolded alongside US-supported stabilization efforts and the passage of a February 20 amnesty law that has facilitated the release of dozens of political prisoners. title: Joy and heartbreak as World Cup qualifying marathon ends
description: The 48-team field for FIFA ’s biggest ever World Cup is complete after a qualifying process that spanned more than two and a half years.  Iraq edged Bolivia 2-1 in an intercontinental playoff at Monterrey in northern Mexico on Tuesday to secure the 48th spot, hours after Bosnia and Herzegovina upset four-time champion Italy on penalties in European playoffs. The result meant Italy was knocked out in qualifying for a third successive World Cup.

title: French truck drivers protest over rising fuel prices
description: Coaches and trucks travelling at less than 30 kilometres per hour: French lorry drivers are taking part in a go-slow operation across the country to protest against soaring fuel prices caused by the war in the Middle East and to demand more substantial support from the government.

title: France's Macron and Japan's Takaichi give nod to 'Dragon Ball' franchise

title: Jailed Palestinians fear death by hanging without due process under new Israeli law
description: Palestinians in the occupied West Bank voiced fears on Tuesday that their jailed relatives could be hanged without due process after Israel adopted a new law ​making the death penalty the default sentence for Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks. The law would also apply to Israeli citizens, but by defining the lethal attacks in question as those "negating Israel's existence" it would be very unlikely that it would be used against Jewish Isra

According to Mr. OWEIS, the humanitarian situation in the region is deteriorating at an alarming pace, particularly for children. What we are witnessing is not only an escalation in violence, but the normalisation of suffering: a condition in which displacement, trauma, and the loss of basic services are becoming embedded in everyday life. title: Europe must reinvent warfare for ‘era of shocks,’ NATO’s Vandier says
description: “We need to make Russia wake up each morning, thinking to themselves: 'Not today,'" the alliance's top officer for force transformation said.

title: Death penalty bill an 'extension of the genocidal measures of the State of Israel': Palestinian FM
description: Speaking to FRANCE 24, Palestinian Foreign Minister Varsen Aghabekian condemned Israel’s new death penalty bill as “an extension of the genocidal measures of the State of Israel”. She accused settlers and the Israeli army of being “one and the same”, and insisted that the two-state solution remains the “only solution”.

title: Israel says will reduce French defence imports 'to zero'
description: Israel will reduce its military imports from France "to zero" in retaliation for French policies it deems hostile towards its interests, a defence ministry spokeswoman told AFP on Tuesday. According to the French parliament, Paris does not export weapons to Israel, but components to be used in defence systems or to be re-exported to third countries. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

title: Bedouins in Israel’s Negev desert face bomb shelter shortage
description: As Iranian and Hezbollah air strikes on Israel continue, tens of thousands of Bedouins living in the country’s southern Negev desert have no access to shelters. While some NGOs are attempting to install mobile shelters, they are primarily calling on the government to take action, denouncing "discriminatory planning policies".

title: French Film Show: ‘Mrs’ lays bare bourgeois family tensions
description: The weight of tradition bears heavily on Marianne, the protagonist of the new film "Mrs", played by Mélanie Thierry as a seemingly perfect wife and mother. Film critic Manon Kerjean explains how director David Roux’s minimalist approach draws on the legacy of bourgeois drama master Claude Chabrol – and why Thierry’s performance carries the film.

title: Eurovision Song Contest expanding with Asian edition
description: The music spectacle Eurovision is holding its first Asian edition in Bangkok later this year. The Eurovision Song Contest Asia 2026 has confirmed artists from at least 10 countries across Asia competing: Thailand, South Korea, the Philippines, Vietnam, Malaysia, Cambodia, Laos, Bangladesh, Nepal and Bhutan. More are expected to join before the finale in November.

title: Transatlantic rift widens as Trump lashes out at NATO
description: President Donald Trump has said he is strongly considering pulling the US out of NATO, ratcheting up his criticism of European allies and exposing a wider rift in the transatlantic alliance — this time over America's war alongside Israel against Iran.

A US effort to seize Kharg Island, the Islamic Republic’s critical economic hub, would be a high-risk operation that would likely entail significant costs for little gain. Kharg is much more likely to become a liability than an asset, drawing the Trump administration closer to the war of attrition it is trying to avoid.

US-Israeli attacks on Iran and Tehran's retaliatory strikes have roiled markets and triggered a surge in energy prices.

title: US Trump says will be leaving Iran 'very soon'

The US–Israeli ‌war with Iran ​has rattled global markets and effectively shut the ​Strait of ​Hormuz, a ​conduit for a ​fifth of global oil and liquefied natural gas flows.

title: Trump says the US could end the Iran war in two to three weeks
description: President Donald ​Trump said the United States could end its military attacks on Iran within two to three weeks and Tehran did not have to make a deal as a prerequisite for the conflict to wind down. The remarks underscored the shifting and at times contradictory statements from Washington about how the war, now in its fifth week, might end.

title: War in the Middle East: Israeli strikes kill 7 in Lebanon
description: Israeli strikes in south Beirut and a nearby area killed seven people and wounded dozens, Lebanon's health ministry said on Wednesday. Lebanon was drawn into the Middle East war on March 2 when Hezbollah launched attacks on Israel to avenge the killing of Iran's leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Israel has responded with broad strikes across Lebanon and a ground offensive.

title: The latest from Tehran: 'Air strikes hit non-military targets every day'
description: The Israeli military said it carried out "a wide-scale wave of strikes targeting infrastructure sites" on Wednesday on Tehran, where Iranian state broadcaster IRIB reported blasts in several areas. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.

title: 'Africa fashion' opens in Paris, celebrates rapid growth of African design scene
description: After dazzling crowds at the V&A in London, the ‘Africa Fashion’ exhibition has landed at the Quai Branly museum in Paris. Our reporters get an insight into this showcase of continental style, from the ancestral weaves of traditional dress to the African creations making a splash on contemporary red carpets.

title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Iraq
description: As a freelance journalist, Shelly #Kittleson has extensively covered #Syria and #Iraq. She was abducted in the heart of #Baghdad, with the kidnappers thought to be #Iran-backed militia Kataeb Hezbollah.

title: #Israel: Champagne in the Knesset as #death penalty law for #Palestinians passes
description: Far-right lawmakers in Israel’s Knesset were seen cheering and celebrating with champagne on Monday after a law was passed that would allow the execution by hanging of Palestinians convicted on terror charges for deadly attacks. The law has triggered widespread condemnation from rights groups and was labelled "cruel and discriminatory” by a UN spokesperson.

title: Iran war economic shocks will last 'months', says Australia's PM
description: Anthony Albanese used a rare TV address to tell Australians that the "months ahead may not be easy" amid the global effects of the war.

title: Thousands lose their jobs in deep cuts at tech giant Oracle
description: It is thought that thousands of people may have lost their jobs at Oracle, one of the world's largest tech companies.

FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Rochelle Ferguson Bouyahi tells us more.

title: Lebanon says Israeli strikes on Beirut area kill 7

description:
Washington is considering ground operations as joint US-Israeli strikes impact Iran’s military and internal security forces, reportedly causing thousands of casualties. Simultaneously, Tehran has increased security recruitment efforts, deployed foreign proxy militias, and escalated executions and arrests of Iranian citizens while issuing threats over “sharing images or footage with foreign actors.”

title: USS Tripoli, embarked 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit arrive in Middle East
description: The Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group and the embarked 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit entered CENTCOM waters Friday.

title: Japanese destroyer can now fire Tomahawk missiles, extending nation’s combat punch
description: Japan’s Tomahawk orders are unlikely to be affected immediately, but longer military campaigns in the Middle East could cause setbacks, one analyst said.

title: Trump tells allies ‘get your own oil’, says Iran war could end in 2-3 weeks
description: Under pressure as domestic petrol prices soar, US president says no deal needed to end war on Iran.

title: Trump says US will be leaving Iran in two to three weeks
description: Speaking to reporters in the Oval Office, US President Donald Trump says US could leave Iran “in two or three weeks.

title: Iran war live: Trump says deal ‘irrelevant’; steel plants in Isfahan hit
description: US-Israeli strikes continue across Iran, hitting pharmaceutical companies and steel plants in Isfahan and Farokhshar.

title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump Says U.S. Campaign to Wind Down and Plans National Address
description: “We will be leaving very soon,” the president told reporters in the Oval Office. A few hours later, the White House said Mr. Trump would provide “an important update on Iran” on Wednesday evening.

title: Israel Strikes Tehran Pharmaceutical Plant, Claiming Military Link
description: Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, said on Tuesday that Israel was “openly and unashamedly bombing pharmaceutical companies.”

title: Johnson digs in after GOP resistance torpedoes DHS funding deal
description: House Speaker Mike Johnson is inching closer to hard red lines on future negotiations over the Department of Homeland Security."They sent us a bill that literally put the number zero in the bill for the funding of border security and customs and immigration enforcement. We can't do that," Johnson (R-La.) said Tuesday on Fox News.At the same time, President Trump is weighing whether to use a rare constitutional power to force Congress to return ne

title: Lapid: Netanyahu incapable of reaching victory, is dismantling Israel from within
description:
Opposition leader rejects PM's claim that Israel has inflicted '10 plagues' on Iran-led axis, noting that Hamas still rules Gaza, Hezbollah continues to fire rockets and that a Khamenei heads Iran
The post Lapid: Netanyahu incapable of reaching victory, is dismantling Israel from within appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Funerals held for 3 of the soldiers killed fighting Hezbollah in southern Lebanon
description:
'My son is a hero, but I don't want a hero for a son,' says father of Staff Sgt. Maxsim Entis, 21; Cpt. Noam Madmoni, 22, Staff Sgt. Ben Cohen, 21, also buried in separate funerals
The post Funerals held for 3 of the soldiers killed fighting Hezbollah in southern Lebanon appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Pope Leo urges Trump to end war on Iran
description: Pope Leo XIV has urged US President Donald Trump to find an “off-ramp” from the US and Israel’s war on Iran.

title: US exempts Gulf of Mexico oil drillers from endangered species protections
description: The Trump administration says protections would diminish US energy competitiveness amid the ongoing war on Iran.

title: EU warns oil and gas prices won’t quickly return to normal after Iran war
description: The EU’s energy commissioner has warned oil and gas prices will not return to normal any time soon.

title: 'You're no longer my sister' - rows erupt as war divides Iranian families
description: People in Iran describe angry scenes and tense relationships as rifts open up over the war.

title: Putin’s Internet Blackout: A Chaotic Drive to Cut Off Russians From the World
description: With new outages and blockages, President Vladimir V. Putin is taking his boldest steps yet to control Russians’ communications.

title: Europe Has a ‘Guns vs. Butter’ Problem. War in Iran Makes It Worse.
description: After decades of prioritizing domestic over military spending, the continent’s leaders are trying to pivot. That is straining national budgets and could anger voters.

title: U.S. Senators Press Taiwan to Raise Military Spending, as China Protests
description: Four visiting senators urged Taiwan to break an impasse over a $40 billion budget proposal, highlighting concerns in Washington about the threat from China.
